title: Request for Quotation (RFQ) - GlowBite 18-Month Go-to-Market Marketing Services Program
scope_summary: Luminos Consumer Brands invites qualified agencies to submit commercial quotations for the 18-month go-to-market launch program for GlowBite, a children's vitamin gummy range for ages 4-12. The RFQ covers strategy, creative development, TVC development and production, organic social content, paid media planning and buying, children's advertising and claims compliance, and end-to-end launch program management. Work is expected to commence in Q4 2026, with initial launch-market deliverables required ahead of a 12-week campaign window beginning in January 2027. Agencies may bid for one, several, or all line items; however, Luminos reserves the right to award line items to multiple vendors. All proposals must include detailed scope assumptions, itemised third-party costs, timeline commitments, resource plans, risk controls, and explicit compliance capability for child-directed advertising and product-claims substantiation.

line_items.name: Strategy & Creative Development
line_items.description: Development of GlowBite launch strategy and creative foundation across both consumer and trade audiences. Scope includes brand narrative, target audience segmentation, shopper and parent insight synthesis, brand positioning, messaging architecture, campaign platform, creative route development, and launch communications framework for retail sell-in and consumer activation.
deliverables: Kick-off workshop with Luminos brand, sales, regulatory, and procurement stakeholders
Audience segmentation framework covering parents/caregivers, gift purchasers, and trade stakeholders
Brand narrative and positioning statement for GlowBite launch market
Messaging architecture including mandatory claims language, proof points, and tone-of-voice guardrails
Trade communications framework for retailer and distributor presentations
Consumer campaign concept deck with minimum 3 distinct creative routes for review
Refined preferred creative route after first-round client feedback
Brand playbook including campaign platform, visual direction, key messages, channel role summary, and do/don't guidance
Launch tagline and core copy lines for TV, digital video, social, OOH, and trade use
Final recommendation presentation delivered in editable and PDF formats

line_items.name: TVC Development
line_items.description: Creative development of television commercial assets derived from the approved campaign platform. Scope includes concept adaptation for film, scriptwriting, storyboard development, animatic recommendation if required, director shortlist and treatment briefing, and casting brief preparation. This line item excludes physical production and post-production execution.
deliverables: Creative briefing document for film adaptation aligned to approved campaign concept
3 initial TVC concept territories mapped to target audience and mandatory claim constraints
Scriptwriting for 3 x 30-second TVCs and 1 x 60-second brand film, including revision rounds
Frame-by-frame storyboard for each approved film asset
Optional low-fidelity animatic recommendation and scene timing guide
Director shortlist with 2-3 written treatments for client review
Casting brief covering minimum 2 on-screen child roles plus parent/caregiver role requirements
Shot list and production rationale for handoff to selected production partner
Final approved script package cleared for compliance review prior to production

line_items.name: TVC Production
line_items.description: Full production and post-production of GlowBite launch film assets following approved scripts and compliance clearance. Scope includes pre-production planning, child and adult talent casting, location sourcing, shoot execution, edit, motion graphics, supers, colour grade, audio mix, legal text integration, and digital cutdowns. All nutritional, efficacy, and implied benefit claims must be substantiated prior to shoot; no health or medical claims may appear without regulatory clearance sign-off.
deliverables: Pre-production schedule, call sheets, production book, and risk assessment
Casting of child and adult talent, including usage rights schedule and guardian consent documentation
Location scouting and permit management for approved shoot locations
One principal shoot covering 3 x 30-second TVCs and 1 x 60-second brand film
Offline edit and 2 review rounds per master asset
Final online masters for 3 x 30-second TVCs and 1 x 60-second brand film
6 x digital cutdowns in mixed durations suitable for paid social and online video
Final colour grade, audio mix, legal supers, and closed-caption files where required
Master delivery in broadcast-ready and digital-ready formats with traffic specifications
Claims substantiation file cross-referenced to each script line and on-screen text element

line_items.name: Social Organic Content
line_items.description: Development of GlowBite organic social presence for launch period across Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. Scope includes channel role definition, content planning, short-form asset creation, copywriting, posting guidance, community management playbook, and influencer identification brief for nano/micro creators suitable for family and parenting contexts.
deliverables: Platform-specific social content strategy for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts
12-week editorial calendar aligned to launch campaign phases
3-month content bank containing minimum 36 finished content pieces across static, carousel, and short-form video formats
Caption copy, hashtag guidance, and call-to-action recommendations for all approved posts
Template library for reactive/community content
Community management playbook including escalation rules for product, safety, and claims-related questions
Influencer identification brief for 5-8 nano/micro creators, including audience fit criteria and child-directed content safeguards
Monthly content performance summary with engagement rate, saves, shares, and top-performing themes
Editable source files and asset usage log

line_items.name: Paid Media Planning
line_items.description: Integrated paid media planning for the 12-week launch window spanning TV, digital, and OOH. Scope includes budget allocation by channel, audience strategy, market weighting, timing and phasing, media rationale, KPI framework, reach/frequency modelling, and TV GRP target planning. The agency must define assumptions clearly and separate strategy/planning fees from media investment.
deliverables: Integrated 12-week media plan across TV, digital video, paid social, search where applicable, and OOH
Channel-mix rationale linked to target audiences, launch objectives, and creative roles
Projected reach and frequency by channel with methodology notes
TV schedule recommendation with minimum GRP target and flighting rationale
Monthly pacing schedule and investment phasing for the full launch window
Audience targeting framework including parent/caregiver targeting approach and safeguards for under-13 audiences
Measurement framework covering awareness, engagement, traffic, and conversion proxy KPIs
Plan version revised after one consolidated Luminos feedback round
Final planning deck and budget spreadsheet in editable format

line_items.name: Paid Media Buying
line_items.description: Execution of the approved paid media plan, including buying, trafficking, optimisation, reconciliation, and reporting. Agency will manage media spend on behalf of Luminos and must pass through all media owner invoices with full transparency. Agency must disclose all rebates, incentives, and commercial arrangements with media owners. Reporting must include CPL, CTR, viewability, and brand-safety incidents. Any digital placements involving audiences under 13 must be designed and trafficked in a COPPA-compliant manner.
deliverables: Media buying and trafficking across approved TV, digital, and OOH placements during the 12-week launch window
Insertion orders, booking confirmations, and placement logs for all channels
Campaign setup document detailing targeting parameters, exclusions, and brand-safety controls
Weekly optimisation summary during active campaign flight
Monthly performance report including CPL, CTR, viewability, delivery vs plan, and brand-safety incidents
Quarterly gross-to-net reconciliation within 30 days of each quarterly media flight
Copies of media owner invoices passed through at cost
Post-campaign wrap report with delivery analysis, learnings, and recommendations for next-phase spend
Signed disclosure statement of all rebates, volume bonuses, kickbacks, and commercial arrangements affecting media cost
Minimum TV delivery commitment of 90% of agreed booked GRPs, with make-good plan required for any shortfall

line_items.name: Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance
line_items.description: Independent or embedded compliance review of all campaign assets and targeting practices relating to children ages 4-12. Scope includes pre-production review, substantiation review for nutritional and product benefit claims, digital privacy controls for under-13 audiences, review against COPPA requirements in the US, CAP/BCAP Code considerations for any UK-facing media, and CARU-aligned guidance for child-directed creative. Written sign-off is mandatory before any asset proceeds to production or trafficking.
deliverables: Compliance framework document covering TV, digital video, social, influencer, OOH, and landing-page asset types
Asset-specific compliance checklist templates for scripts, storyboards, social posts, paid media audiences, and influencer briefs
Written sign-off memo for each TVC prior to production
Written sign-off memo for digital and social asset batches prior to publication or trafficking
Claims substantiation review log linking each approved claim to source documentation
COPPA risk review for any data collection, targeting, or engagement mechanism involving under-13 users
Quarterly compliance audit report covering launched assets and media execution
Issue escalation protocol and remediation guidance for non-compliant materials
Final compliance archive package for Luminos records

line_items.name: Launch Program Management
line_items.description: Central project management office for the 18-month GlowBite launch program, coordinating Luminos stakeholders and appointed agencies across strategy, production, media, compliance, and reporting workstreams. Scope includes workback planning, governance, dependency tracking, risk management, decision logging, weekly and monthly reporting, and launch-readiness control through the first campaign phase.
deliverables: Master project plan in Gantt format covering all line items and decision gates
Detailed workback schedule from contract award through launch and post-launch review
Weekly status report covering timeline, budget status, decisions required, and risks
Weekly cross-agency coordination meeting agenda and action log
Monthly executive summary for Luminos leadership
Risk register updated fortnightly with owners, mitigation actions, and impact assessment
Change control log for scope, budget, and schedule adjustments
Launch readiness checklist for pre-flight approvals and asset handoff gates
Post-launch retrospective report with lessons learned and next-phase recommendations

commercial_expectations: Luminos Consumer Brands expects commercially robust and transparent proposals. Preferred payment structure for each awarded line item is 30% at contract signature, 30% upon completion of an agreed mid-point milestone tied to defined deliverables, and 40% upon final delivery sign-off; payment in full at project commencement will not be accepted. Bidders must specify milestone definitions by line item in their response. All quoted fees must be inclusive of agency profit margin, management fees, and overhead; no undisclosed markups on third-party costs are permitted. Third-party costs must be itemised separately and clearly labelled as pass-through or managed service costs. For paid media buying, all media owner invoices must be passed through to Luminos and any service fee, commission, or optimisation fee must be separately stated. The media buying agency must provide gross-to-net reconciliation within 30 days of each quarterly media flight. Vendors must disclose whether pricing is based on project fee, retainer, day rate, commission, or hybrid model, and must state assumptions on number of revision rounds, included meetings, and out-of-scope triggers. Vendors must hold professional indemnity insurance of not less than USD 2,000,000 per occurrence and provide evidence prior to contract award. Contracted service levels will include milestone dates; a 15% performance penalty applies to the relevant invoice for missed agreed milestones attributable to the vendor, unless delay is caused by documented Luminos approval hold, force majeure, or approved scope change. For TV buying and delivery, agencies must commit to a minimum of 90% of booked GRP delivery and provide make-goods or fee relief for under-delivery. Luminos reserves the right to negotiate final scope, split awards across agencies, request best-and-final-offer pricing, and reject any proposal that does not meet compliance, transparency, or insurance requirements.
questionnaire: Please describe your proposed pricing model for each line item bid (project fee, retainer, day rate, commission, or hybrid) and specify exactly what is included and excluded in the quoted fee.
Provide a detailed line-item cost breakdown, separating agency fees, production costs, talent/usage, post-production, media fees, and all other third-party pass-through costs.

Please provide the proposed account team structure for this engagement, including named leads, functional roles, seniority levels, percentage allocation, and whether resources are dedicated or shared across other clients.

Describe your agency's experience in FMCG and/or children's product marketing. Include at least three relevant examples of comparable work, the scope delivered, campaign scale, and the specific role played by your team.

Do you currently serve any client that could create a conflict of interest with GlowBite in the children's food, beverage, supplement, wellness, or broader FMCG categories? Please explain your conflicts policy and how conflicts are monitored and managed.

Describe how you manage timeline risk and scope changes that may affect launch date. Include your escalation process, dependency tracking method, and approach to recovery planning when milestones slip.

Please outline your in-house and subcontracted expertise in children's advertising and claims compliance. Specify which regulatory frameworks your team is experienced with, including COPPA, CAP/BCAP, CARU, and product-claims substantiation review.

For paid media respondents: describe your media transparency policy, including disclosure of rebates, volume incentives, inventory arrangements, and how gross-to-net reconciliation is produced and validated.

Please confirm your willingness to accept the proposed payment structure of 30% at contract signature, 30% at mid-point milestone, and 40% at final delivery sign-off. If exceptions are requested, provide reasons and proposed alternatives.
Please state your standard revision rounds for strategy, creative, scripts, edits, and reporting deliverables, and identify what would trigger incremental fees.
Provide your quality assurance process for claim accuracy, mandatory legal text, and version control across TV, digital, social, influencer, and OOH assets.

Please identify any elements of the scope you would propose to subcontract. For each subcontracted area, name the function, location, oversight model, and quality-control approach without referencing proprietary third-party technologies.
Provide two client references for accounts of comparable scale and category, including company name, contact role, email address, phone number, and scope delivered.
Please confirm your professional indemnity insurance coverage amount, insurer type, and renewal date, and attach proof of coverage with your response.
compliance_requirements: All creative content targeting children under 13 must comply with COPPA (US) and applicable advertising codes before production. A qualified compliance officer's sign-off is mandatory before shoot.
Product efficacy and nutritional claims must be substantiated with documented evidence prior to inclusion in any script, visual, or media asset. No implied health claims without regulatory clearance.

Media agency must provide a signed disclosure of all media owner rebates, volume bonuses, or commercial arrangements that affect the net media rate. Failure to disclose is grounds for contract termination.
Agency must maintain and make available on request a conflict-of-interest register covering all clients in the children's food/beverage and FMCG categories.
Data privacy: any digital campaign element targeting users under 13 must be designed for COPPA compliance with written confirmation from the agency's legal or compliance function.
No TVC, digital video, social content batch, influencer brief, or paid media audience setup may proceed to production or trafficking without documented compliance review and written approval.
If any UK media is proposed, all applicable child-directed creative and scheduling must be reviewed against CAP/BCAP Code requirements before booking.
All child talent use must include appropriate guardian consent, usage-rights documentation, and safeguarding procedures appropriate to age and production setting.

Agencies must retain claims substantiation files, approval logs, and final compliance sign-off records for not less than 24 months after campaign completion and make them available to Luminos on request.
Any identified compliance breach, data privacy issue, or claim challenge must be reported to Luminos within one business day together with remediation steps and exposure assessment.
